title: APUSH content vocabulary — periods x themes grid
note: The content spine the generator ties every item to. A generated item is tagged (period, theme). Periods and weights are from the College Board CED; key_concepts are brief teacher-level summaries for seeding, not verbatim CED text. Ranges overlap by design (CED periodization).
source: College Board AP U.S. History Course and Exam Description (see docs/sources.md [CED-content])

themes.name: American and National Identity
themes.scope: citizenship, constitutional debates, nationalism vs regionalism, definitions of American identity

themes.name: Work, Exchange, and Technology
themes.scope: labor systems, capitalism, trade, technology, economic policy

themes.name: Geography and the Environment
themes.scope: land, resources, environment, expansion, settlement geography

themes.name: Migration and Settlement
themes.scope: immigration, internal migration, settlement patterns, demographic change

themes.name: Politics and Power
themes.scope: government, parties, reform movements, political ideologies, federal-state power

themes.name: America in the World
themes.scope: diplomacy, war, empire, foreign policy, global role

themes.name: American and Regional Culture
themes.scope: religion, arts, ideas, regional and group cultures, reform culture

themes.name: Social Structures
themes.scope: class, race, gender, family, hierarchy, social movements

key_concepts: Pre-contact Native societies and environmental adaptation
Spanish colonization and the encomienda/casta system
early European-Native contact and the Columbian debate
regional colonial models (New England, Chesapeake, Middle, Southern)
Seven Years' War and imperial reorganization
Jeffersonian vs Federalist visions; Marshall Court
Second Great Awakening and antebellum reform
Manifest Destiny and expansion; Mexican-American War
sectional crisis (Compromise of 1850, Kansas-Nebraska, Dred Scott)
industrialization, big business, and the Gilded Age
Western settlement and Native displacement (Dawes Act)
Great Depression and the New Deal
WWII mobilization and its social effects
Cold War: containment, Korea, Vietnam, detente
Reagan conservatism and the end of the Cold War
